figures.caption: Table 3. The bug discovery methods already used by developers of each system.
Figure 1. An example of a buggy Rust function simplified from a C compiler [3] developed by Anthropic.

Figure 6. The reasoning process of the function from_keyword in Figure 1. For brevity, we only show the code fragment that contains the bug. Every comment is the post-condition of the preceding code and the pre-condition of the following code.

Figure 5. An example of the top-down paradigm for specification generation. Each directed edge from function 𝐹𝑖 to function 𝐹 𝑗 indicates that 𝐹𝑖 invokes 𝐹 𝑗 . The specification of 𝐹 𝑗 is generated based on the expected specification from all its callers 𝐹𝑖 , which is denoted as 𝐹𝑖 ⊢ {𝑃}𝐹 𝑗 {𝑄} on the edge.
